title: Raw Earthy Polish Vegetables Pierogi
description: A dairy-free-friendly take on traditional Polish pierogi, featuring a miso-marinated raw vegetable filling, perfect for a comforting earthy meal.

ingredients.name: apple cider vinegar
ingredients.notes: mixed with miso paste for marinating

instructions: Grate the beets, carrots, parsnips, and celery root, and mix together in a bowl.
In a separate bowl, mix the miso paste and apple cider vinegar to create a marinade.
Add the onion powder and garlic powder to the marinade and stir to combine.
Add the grated vegetables to the marinade and mix until well coated.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to allow the vegetables to marinate.
While the vegetables are marinating, mix together the wheat flour and water in a separate bowl to create a dough.
Knead the dough for 5-10 minutes until it is smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into 8-10 equal pieces and roll each piece into a ball.
Roll out each ball of dough into a thin circle, about 1/8 inch thick.
Place 1-2 tablespoons of the marinated vegetable mixture in the center of each dough circle.
Fold the dough over the filling and press the edges together to seal the pierogi.
Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil and cook the pierogi for 5-7 minutes, or until they float to the surface.
Drain the pierogi and serve hot with dairy-free cheese alternative, topped with activated charcoal powder for a cheesy pull.
If desired, add additional toppings such as chopped fresh herbs or a dollop of dairy-free sour cream.
